issues.title: Revenue includes FAILED/PENDING transactions
issues.line: SUM(ft.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY ft.customer_id) AS customer_lifetime_value
issues.problem: The query calculates customer lifetime value without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN ft.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN ft.amount ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ft.customer_id) AS customer_lifetime_value

issues.title: Correlated subquery instead of JOIN
issues.line: (SELECT email FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc WHERE dc.customer_id = ft.customer_id) AS customer_email
issues.problem: Using a correlated subquery can be inefficient. A JOIN would be more performant.
issues.fix:
JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id
dc.email AS customer_email

issues.title: Unnecessarily exposes customer email
issues.line: (SELECT email FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc WHERE dc.customer_id = ft.customer_id) AS customer_email
issues.problem: Exposing customer email can lead to PII exposure.
issues.fix: Remove customer_email from the SELECT clause or mask it if necessary.

issues.title: No table aliases on subquery, unclear variable names
issues.line: (SELECT email FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc WHERE dc.customer_id = ft.customer_id) AS customer_email
issues.problem: The subquery lacks table aliases and the variable names are not descriptive.
issues.fix: Use aliases and descriptive variable names.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT
    ft.transaction_id,
    ft.amount,
    ft.status,
    dm.merchant_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ft.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN ft.amount ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ft.customer_id) AS customer_lifetime_value
FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft
JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id
ORDER BY customer_lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with correctness, performance, security, and readability. Corrected the revenue calculation, replaced the correlated subquery with a JOIN, removed customer email, and improved readability.

issues.title: Missing comment on logic
issues.problem: The SQL query lacks comments explaining the logic behind the CASE statement and the GROUP BY clause.
issues.fix: -- Calculating revenue by summing completed transactions

issues.title: Potential incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The revenue calculation might be incorrect if there are NULL values in the 'amount' column. It should handle NULLs explicitly.
issues.fix: SUM(COALESCE(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END, 0))

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The alias 't' for 'fact_transactions' and 'm' for 'dim_merchant' is not consistently used in comments.
issues.fix: -- Using 't' for transactions and 'm' for merchants

review.corrected_sql:
-- Sample Query 1: Total revenue by merchant
SELECT 
    m.merchant_name,
    SUM(COALESCE(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END, 0)) AS revenue
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY revenue DESC;
-- Calculating revenue by summing completed transactions
-- Using 't' for transactions and 'm' for merchants
review.summary: The query is mostly correct but could benefit from additional comments and handling of potential NULL values in the 'amount' column.

issues.title: Incorrect failure rate calculation
issues.line: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) as failure_rate_pct
issues.problem: The failure rate calculation is incorrect because it uses COUNT(*) which includes all transactions, not just COMPLETED ones.
issues.fix: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) as failure_rate_pct

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
-- Sample Query 2: Transaction failure rate by payment method
SELECT 
    payment_method,
    COUNT(*) as total_transactions,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) as failure_rate_pct
FROM fact_transactions
GROUP BY payment_method
ORDER BY failure_rate_pct DESC;
review.summary: The query has a correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and lacks comments for readability.

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.
issues.fix:
-- Sample Query 3: Customer transaction counts by tier
SELECT 
    dc.tier,
    COUNT(*) as transaction_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT dc.customer_id) as unique_customers,
    AVG(ft.amount) as avg_transaction_amount
FROM fact_transactions ft
JOIN dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id
WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY dc.tier
ORDER BY transaction_count DESC;

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The aliases used for the tables are not consistent with typical naming conventions (e.g., 'ft' for 'fact_transactions', 'dc' for 'dim_customer').
issues.fix:
-- Sample Query 3: Customer transaction counts by tier
SELECT 
    dc.tier,
    COUNT(*) as transaction_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT dc.customer_id) as unique_customers,
    AVG(ft.amount) as avg_transaction_amount
FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft
JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id
WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY dc.tier
ORDER BY transaction_count DESC;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Sample Query 3: Customer transaction counts by tier
SELECT 
    dc.tier,
    COUNT(*) as transaction_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT dc.customer_id) as unique_customers,
    AVG(ft.amount) as avg_transaction_amount
FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft
JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id
WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY dc.tier
ORDER BY transaction_count DESC;
review.summary: The query is logically correct and performs well, but readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming conventions.
